basic_details.short_information: This section gives a quick snapshot of the IBPS PO/MT-XVI Bharti 2026 for candidates who want the key facts at a glance. The Common Recruitment Process (CRP) is conducted once a year by IBPS on behalf of 11 public sector banks for vacancies arising in the financial year 2027-28.

application_fee.details: The application fee for IBPS PO/MT-XVI Recruitment 2026 depends on the candidate's category and must be paid online between 01 July 2026 and 21 July 2026. Bank transaction charges for online payment are to be borne by the candidate separately. | Payment can be made through Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, Mobile Wallets, or UPI. Candidates should note that the fee applicable for modifying the application during the Edit Window is an additional ₹200/-, which is non-refundable and uniformly applicable to all categories.

qualification.details: Candidates wondering about the IBPS PO Eligibility Criteria 2026 can note that both age and educational qualification requirements must be fulfilled by the specified cut-off dates. The eligibility criteria mentioned in the notification are the basic requirements for applying, and final eligibility is subject to verification by IBPS and the participating banks at every stage of the recruitment process. | The minimum age required is 20 years and the maximum age is 30 years, meaning candidates must be born not earlier than 02.07.1996 and not later than 01.07.2006. Age relaxation is available for reserved category candidates as detailed below. | Candidates should hold a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a University recognised by the Government of India, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government, as on 21.07.2026. The candidate must possess a valid mark-sheet or degree certificate confirming graduation on the day of registration and must indicate the percentage of marks obtained in Graduation, calculated to the nearest two decimals.

salary_details.details: Candidates asking about the IBPS PO Salary 2026 can refer to the official pay scale specified for the Probationary Officer/Management Trainee post. The basic pay structure is ₹48,480-2000/7-62,480-2340/2-67,160-2680/7-85,920, and selected officials will also be eligible for allowances and perquisites as per the rules of the participating bank in force from time to time. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: The IBPS PO/MT-XVI Selection Process 2026 consists of four stages: Online Preliminary Examination
and Common Interview. Only candidates who qualify at each stage move forward to the next
and provisional allotment is done based on the final combined score and vacancies reported by the banks.
There is a penalty of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer in the objective tests
while unattempted questions carry no penalty.
Candidates who qualify the Main Examination are called for a self-report Personality Test
which is non-qualifying in nature but mandatory to attend for interview eligibility. The Personality Test may be conducted in proctored mode or at designated examination centres

with minimum qualifying marks of 40% (35% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates). The final combined score is calculated using a weightage ratio of 80:20 between the Main Examination and Interview respectively.
how_to_apply: Candidates who want to know how to complete the IBPS PO/MT-XVI Apply Online process can follow the steps below. Applications can only be submitted online through the official IBPS website; no other mode of application is accepted.
Visit the official website www.ibps.in and click on "CRP PO/MT" on the homepage.
Click "CLICK HERE TO APPLY ONLINE FOR CRP-PROBATIONARY OFFICERS/MANAGEMENT TRAINEES (CRP-PO/MT-XVI)".
Click "CLICK HERE FOR NEW REGISTRATION" and enter basic details to generate a provisional registration number and password.
Upload photograph, signature, left thumb impression, hand-written declaration, and SSC/SSLC/10th standard certificate as per the specified format and size.
Fill in all details carefully using the "SAVE AND NEXT" facility before final submission, since no changes are allowed after clicking "COMPLETE REGISTRATION".
Pay the applicable application fee/intimation charges online.
Take a printout of the e-receipt and the completed online application form for future reference.

content_sections.heading: IBPS CRP PO/MT-XVI Notification PDF Download
content_sections.text: Candidates looking for the IBPS PO/MT-XVI Notification PDF Download link can access the official detailed notification directly from the IBPS website before applying. The detailed notification titled "Detailed-Notification_CRP-PO-XVI_Final_V1_30_06_2026" was released on 01 July 2026 and signed by the Director, IBPS, Mumbai. It contains complete information on eligibility, exam pattern, vacancy distribution, and application procedure, and candidates are strongly advised to read it thoroughly before registering online since the eligibility criteria specified are basic requirements only, and final eligibility is subject to verification by the participating banks. Download IBPS CRP PO/MT-XVI Notification PDF Here Direct Link to Apply Online for IBPS CRP PO/MT-XVI

content_sections.heading: IBPS PO/MT-XVI Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: This section gives a quick snapshot of the IBPS PO/MT-XVI Bharti 2026 for candidates who want the key facts at a glance. The Common Recruitment Process (CRP) is conducted once a year by IBPS on behalf of 11 public sector banks for vacancies arising in the financial year 2027-28.

rows.information: Prelim Exam, Main Exam, Personality Test, Interview

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates planning their preparation schedule can refer to the tentative timeline released by IBPS for the online examination and subsequent stages. The online registration and main examination are separated by roughly three months, giving candidates adequate preparation time between the preliminary and main stages.

rows.activity: Edit Window for Modification of Application
rows.tentative_schedule: For 2 days after closing of registration

content_sections.heading: IBPS PO/MT-XVI Vacancy 2026: Bank-Wise and Category-Wise Breakup
content_sections.text: The IBPS PO/MT-XVI Vacancy 2026 is spread across 11 participating public sector banks, with a total of 6715 indicative vacancies . These vacancies are anticipated figures reported by the participating banks, and the final provisional allotment will depend on actual vacancies confirmed closer to the recruitment cycle. Note: Indian Bank, UCO Bank, and Union Bank of India vacancies are marked "Not Reported (NR)" in the official notification and will be updated separately. Out of the total vacancies, 73 are reserved for Hearing Impaired (HI), 70 for Orthopedically Challenged (OC), 67 for Visually Impaired (VI), and 77 for Intellectual Disability (ID) category candidates under PwBD.

content_sections.heading: IBPS PO/MT-XVI Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the IBPS PO Eligibility Criteria 2026 can note that both age and educational qualification requirements must be fulfilled by the specified cut-off dates. The eligibility criteria mentioned in the notification are the basic requirements for applying, and final eligibility is subject to verification by IBPS and the participating banks at every stage of the recruitment process. The minimum age required is 20 years and the maximum age is 30 years , meaning candidates must be born not earlier than 02.07.1996 and not later than 01.07.2006. Age relaxation is available for reserved category candidates as detailed below. Candidates should hold a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a University recognised by the Government of India, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government, as on 21.07.2026 . The candidate must possess a valid mark-sheet or degree certificate confirming graduation on the day of registration and must indicate the percentage of marks obtained in Graduation, calculated to the nearest two decimals.

content_sections.heading: IBPS PO/MT-XVI Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee for IBPS PO/MT-XVI Recruitment 2026 depends on the candidate's category and must be paid online between 01 July 2026 and 21 July 2026. Bank transaction charges for online payment are to be borne by the candidate separately. Payment can be made through Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, Mobile Wallets, or UPI. Candidates should note that the fee applicable for modifying the application during the Edit Window is an additional ₹200/- , which is non-refundable and uniformly applicable to all categories.

content_sections.heading: IBPS PO/MT-XVI Salary and Pay Scale 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates asking about the IBPS PO Salary 2026 can refer to the official pay scale specified for the Probationary Officer/Management Trainee post. The basic pay structure is ₹48,480-2000/7-62,480-2340/2-67,160-2680/7-85,920 , and selected officials will also be eligible for allowances and perquisites as per the rules of the participating bank in force from time to time. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: IBPS PO/MT-XVI Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: The IBPS PO/MT-XVI Selection Process 2026 consists of four stages: Online Preliminary Examination, Online Main Examination, Personality Test, and Common Interview. Only candidates who qualify at each stage move forward to the next, and provisional allotment is done based on the final combined score and vacancies reported by the banks. There is a penalty of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer in the objective tests, while unattempted questions carry no penalty. Candidates who qualify the Main Examination are called for a self-report Personality Test, which is non-qualifying in nature but mandatory to attend for interview eligibility. The Personality Test may be conducted in proctored mode or at designated examination centres, as decided by IBPS. The interview carries 100 marks , with minimum qualifying marks of 40% (35% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates). The final combined score is calculated using a weightage ratio of 80:20 between the Main Examination and Interview respectively.

tables.caption: Step 2: Online Main Examination (Objective and Descriptive)

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026 (Apply Online)
content_sections.text: Candidates who want to know how to complete the IBPS PO/MT-XVI Apply Online process can follow the steps below. Applications can only be submitted online through the official IBPS website; no other mode of application is accepted. Visit the official website www.ibps.in and click on "CRP PO/MT" on the homepage. Click "CLICK HERE TO APPLY ONLINE FOR CRP-PROBATIONARY OFFICERS/MANAGEMENT TRAINEES (CRP-PO/MT-XVI)". Click "CLICK HERE FOR NEW REGISTRATION" and enter basic details to generate a provisional registration number and password. Upload photograph, signature, left thumb impression, hand-written declaration, and SSC/SSLC/10th standard certificate as per the specified format and size. Fill in all details carefully using the "SAVE AND NEXT" facility before final submission, since no changes are allowed after clicking "COMPLETE REGISTRATION". Pay the applicable application fee/intimation charges online. Take a printout of the e-receipt and the completed online application form for future reference. Direct Link to Apply Online for IBPS CRP PO/MT-XVI

content_sections.heading: Document Requirements for Scanning and Upload
content_sections.text: Candidates must prepare digital scanned copies of specific documents before starting the online application, since the system will not allow progress unless these meet the exact specifications. This includes the photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and handwritten declaration in prescribed dimensions and file sizes. The signature and handwritten declaration must not be in capital letters, and both must be in the candidate's own handwriting, since any mismatch with the signature on the attendance sheet or call letter will lead to disqualification.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates unintentionally get their applications rejected due to avoidable errors during the online registration process. Being aware of these common pitfalls in advance can help candidates complete their IBPS PO/MT-XVI application smoothly. Uploading a signature or handwritten declaration in capital letters, which is not accepted under any circumstances. Entering the 'Name', 'Email ID', 'Mobile Number', or 'Nationality' fields incorrectly, since these cannot be edited after submission. Waiting until the last date to pay fees, which risks failure due to heavy website traffic near the deadline. Uploading blurred or smudged photographs, signatures, or thumb impressions that don't meet the prescribed file size and dimension limits. Selecting the wrong category and later expecting it to be changed after the Edit Window closes. Forgetting to retain multiple copies of the uploaded photograph, which is required at several later stages of the process. Not indicating scribe requirements or compensatory time preferences at the time of registration, since such requests are not entertained later.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply online for IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window for IBPS CRP PO/MT-XVI closes on 21 July 2026, with registration having started on 01 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in IBPS PO/MT-XVI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 6715 indicative vacancies for Probationary Officer/Management Trainee posts across 11 participating banks, though vacancies for Indian Bank, UCO Bank, and Union Bank of India were not reported at the time of notification.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 20 and 30 years as on 01.07.2026, with age relaxation available for SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), PwBD (10 years), and eligible Ex-servicemen (5 years).

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for IBPS PO/MT-XVI?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a recognised university, with the result declared on or before 21 July 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹175/- for SC/ST/PwBD candidates and ₹850/- for all other categories, payable online only.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves four stages: Online Preliminary Examination, Online Main Examination, Personality Test, and Common Interview, followed by provisional allotment to a participating bank.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary of IBPS PO/MT-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: The basic pay scale is ₹48,480-2000/7-62,480-2340/2-67,160-2680/7-85,920, along with allowances and perquisites as per the participating bank's rules.

faq.question: Q8. When will the Preliminary Examination for IBPS PO/MT-XVI be held?
faq.answer: The Online Preliminary Examination is tentatively scheduled for August 2026, with the Main Examination expected in October 2026.

faq.question: Q9. Is there a penalty for wrong answers in IBPS PO/MT-XVI exams?
faq.answer: Yes, 0.25 marks are deducted for each wrong answer in the objective tests, while unattempted questions do not carry any penalty.

faq.question: Q10. Which banks are participating in IBPS CRP PO/MT-XVI 2026?
faq.answer: The 11 participating banks are Bank of Baroda, Bank of India, Bank of Maharashtra, Canara Bank, Central Bank of India, Indian Bank, Indian Overseas Bank, Punjab National Bank, Punjab & Sind Bank, UCO Bank, and Union Bank of India.
